description_long: This is a whimsical, vintage-style biplane painted in a soft pink color with heart motifs on the wings and fuselage, evoking a playful or romantic theme. The aircraft features two stacked wings supported by struts and rigging wires, with a wooden propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is rounded and tapers toward the tail, which includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a visible seat and control area. The landing gear appears to be a simple taildragger configuration, with two main wheels and a small tail skid. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, with a charming, decorative finish.
description: A pink, heart-decorated vintage biplane with an open cockpit, wooden propeller, and classic taildragger landing gear. Its playful design and double wings evoke early aviation with a romantic twist.
description_view_0: Front-top view showing both pink wings with heart motifs, the open cockpit, and the rounded nose with propeller hub visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stacked biplane wings, wooden propeller, and heart motif on the fuselage and upper wing.
description_view_2: Direct front view focusing on the spinning propeller, wing struts, and symmetrical heart decorations on the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tail section, heart motifs, and the open cockpit from a different angle.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and rigging; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with no canopy.
